business.headline: World Bank raises India growth forecast to 6.6% for 2026-27 from 6.3% earlierEven as growth across the region slows, India continues to stand out as the main engine, providing a sense of stability at a time when the broader economic outlook remains uncertain.

Big rally, bigger question: Buy now or wait? | INTERVIEWMarkets rebounded sharply on Wednesday as lower crude prices lifted sentiment. Sumeet Bagadia of Choice Broking discusses if this is a temporary bounce or a trend shift.

Trump warns of 50% tariffs on countries supplying weapons to IranThe statement comes at a time when global geopolitical risks are already high, especially in West Asia. Any move to impose such tariffs could have wider implications for global trade and diplomatic ties.

Borrowing to survive, facing abuse: How India's credit boom is turning stressfulNearly 4 in 10 borrowers report abusive recovery calls, as a new survey highlights rising repayment stress and growing dependence on credit for everyday needs.

Dalal Street adds Rs 17 lakh crore as Iran-US ceasefire boosts marketsInvestor wealth surged by around Rs 17.1 lakh crore in a single session, taking total BSE market capitalisation to Rs 445.8 lakh crore. The S&P BSE Sensex rallied 2,946.32 points to end at 77,562.90, while the NSE Nifty50 jumped 873.35 points to close at 23,997.35.

Resilient, safe, strong: RBI assures banking system safe amid recent jittersRBI Governor highlighted that the broader banking system remains stable despite global challenges. He said there are no systemic risks to the profitability or health of banks, even as global uncertainty remains due to the West Asia conflict.

Why did RBI keep repo rate unchanged at 5.25%? Know these 5 reasonsNo change in the repo rate this time, but the move is far from random. The RBI has carefully weighed multiple factors before deciding to hold at 5.25%. Here are five reasons behind the decision.

Bitcoin above $70,000, altcoins surge up to 23%: Is a new crypto rally starting?The crypto market is showing fresh signs of strength. Bitcoin has reclaimed the $70,000 level, and altcoins are posting strong gains, leaving investors wondering if this momentum can sustain in the coming weeks.

RBI's subtle growth warning is easy to miss, but it's thereRBI's growth warning was subtle, but it was evident in Governor Sanjay Malhotra's monetary policy statement. In this article, we have a closer look at the biggest risks to India's economy.

Sensex, Nifty jump, but IT stocks trade flat. Will they perform post Q4 results?The market rally was broad-based, with strong gains across banking, financial, auto and other sectors. However, IT stocks stood out as the only laggards, trading flat or with marginal gains despite the sharp market rise.

Gold up Rs 5,000, silver jumps Rs 14,000: Will the rally continue?Precious metals are glittering again. In a short span, gold prices have risen by Rs 5,000 and silver has surged Rs 14,000, prompting fresh interest from investors trying to decode whether this momentum will continue.

What is RBI's plan after rupee's recent fall vs US dollar? RBI Governor explainsRBI's focus will remain on managing sharp swings in the currency rather than controlling its direction, as global factors continue to drive movements in the rupee.

RBI keeps repo rate unchanged at 5.25%: How it impacts your EMI and home loan plansIn a welcome move for homebuyers, the RBI has kept the repo rate unchanged at 5.25%. This means no immediate increase in EMIs, offering much-needed relief and stability to borrowers planning their finances.

Elevated energy prices, disruptions at Hormuz likely to impact growth: RBI GuvThe warning comes even as the central bank projects India's economy to grow 6.9% this financial year, signalling that while the growth engine is intact, fresh global shocks are building.

Gold at Rs 1.53 lakh, silver jumps 5%: Why are prices rising today?The rise in precious metals comes alongside a broader rally in equities and a sharp fall in crude oil prices, as investors adjust to changing risk conditions.

RBI keeps key lending rates unchanged at 5.25%, stance remains 'neutral'The Reserve Bank of India has kept its key policy rate unchanged at 5.25%, marking no change since December 2025, as policymakers chose to wait amid rising global uncertainty linked to the West Asia conflict.

Adani moves US court seeking dismissal of SEC fraud case, says lawsuit lacks jurisdictionIn a pre-motion letter filed ahead of a planned April 30 motion seeking dismissal of the lawsuit, Adanis through their lawyers said the SEC's claims over a 2021 bond sale by the group's renewable energy arm, Adani Green Energy Ltd (AGEL) are legally flawed on multiple grounds.
IndiGo shares rally 10%: Why is the stock up today?As of 9:50 am, IndiGo shares were trading at Rs 4,681.50, up 9.68% on the Bombay Stock Exchange (BSE).

Iran-US announce ceasefire: How will it impact RBI MPC decision today?RBI MPC: The ceasefire between the US and Iran, which has led to a sharp fall in crude oil prices, is expected to play a key role in shaping the RBI's stance. Lower oil prices ease pressure on inflation and the overall economy, giving the central bank more room to maintain stability.

entertainment.headline: The Boys Season 5 review: Messy and loud, but bloody unmissable finishRelease Date:Wed Apr 08, 2026 The Boys Season 5 review: The final chapter delivers a gripping but uneven farewell, with Antony Starr's Homelander standing out as the show balances satire, shock and emotional stakes.

NTRNeel update: No delay in Jr NTR and Prashanth Neel's film, claim makers The makers of NTRNeel clarified the misinformation about the film's schedule cancellation. The film is co-produced by Mythri Movie Makers and NTR Arts.

Pawan Kalyan's wife recalls son's Singapore fire scare, probe still on Actor Pawan Kalyan's wife Anna Lezhneva marks one year of son Mark's fire accident in Singapore, calling it his "second birthday", while also celebrating Akira Nandan's 22nd birthday.

Saurabh Shukla calls Shah Rukh Khan a wise father, shares on-set dynamic Actor Saurabh Shukla opened up about Shah Rukh Khan's approach as a father on the sets of King, revealing how the superstar balances guidance and professionalism with Suhana Khan.

Anurag Kashyap on casting Mrunal Thakur: I have something crazy for her in mind Filmmaker Anurag Kashyap has hinted at a bold, unconventional role for actor Mrunal Thakur in an exclusive interview. He also reflected on her standout performances, saying Thakur often underestimates her own abilities.

Cocktail 2 gets messy: Bipasha Basu's repost fuels Shahid Kapoor drama Cocktail 2 brews more than just on-screen chemistry! From Shahid Kapoor's alleged screen-time worries to Bipasha Basu's viral repost, this Bollywood buzz is serving drama, gossip, and a whole lot of masala.

We pitched Dacoit to Anurag Kashyap at Sobhita Dhulipala's wedding, says Adivi Sesh Adivi Sesh shared a fascinating behind-the-scenes anecdote about his upcoming film, Dacoit. The actor revealed that the project was actually pitched to filmmaker Anurag Kashyap in the middle of the celebrations at Sobhita Dhulipala's wedding.

Dhurandhar actor Ashwin Dhar reacts to Arshad Pappu's look being compared to SRK Ashwin Dhar reacted to viral memes comparing his Dhurandhar: The Revenge look to Shah Rukh Khan. He called it a pleasant surprise.

Actor Swastika Mukherjee slams Sourav Ganguly over Rahul Banerjee drowning remark Swastika Mukherjee strongly criticised Sourav Ganguly for his insensitive remarks on actor Rahul Banerjee's tragic drowning during a shoot, highlighting the need for respect towards all professions.

No love notes for Sanya Malhotra and Rishab Rikhiram Sharma anymore? After a year of hush-hush romance, Sanya Malhotra and Rishab Rikhiram Sharma have reportedly broken up. Wondering the reason? Here's everything you need to know about their quiet breakup.

Allu Arjun's 44th birthday draws crowds and fireworks. Watch videos Pushpa star Allu Arjun's fans gathered in huge numbers outside his home to wish him on his 44th birthday. The actor stepped out to massive cheers, fireworks and more as he greeted them.

The Devil Wears Prada 2 final trailer: Anne Hathaway, Meryl Streep in new crisis The trailer for The Devil Wears Prada 2 shows Anne Hathaway and Meryl Streep's reunion for a new crisis. The upcoming film brings back the original stars and introduces new faces.

It's actually fun: Harman Baweja on his job as producer for the series Kaptaan Harman Baweja is the writer-producer of the new series, Kaptaan. The actor, who debuted with Love Story 2050 opposite Priyanka Chopra and made a mark with Hansal Mehta's Scoop, opened up about his shift from acting to producing, and also opened up on the importance of work-life balance.

Rajinikanth refuses to comment on Jana Nayagan delay, shares Jailer 2 update Rajinikanth declined to comment on the delay of Vijay's Jana Nayagan due to CBFC issues. He shared updates on Jailer 2 and RKxKH films and urged youngsters to avoid harmful habits and focus on education.

I was cautious, he was fearless: Aditya Dhar credits Mukesh Chhabra for Dhurandhar Aditya Dhar praised Mukesh Chhabra for shaping Dhurandhar: The Revenge with his fearless vision and dedication. He said Chhabra's casting made the film feel more real, adding that the department is often overlooked despite being a crucial part of filmmaking.

Samay Raina breaks down on stage; recalls fear, guilt and chaos after Latent row Comedian Samay Raina recalled the chaos and emotional turmoil following the vandalism at The Habitat after the Ranveer Allahbadia controversy. He also shared how people associated with him were deeply affected due to what transpired after the show was shut down.

Chiraiya writer reacts to backlash, trolling on marital rape theme: Didn't foresee it Chiraiya writer Divy Nidhi Sharma reacted to backlash and trolling over the show's marital rape theme, saying he didn't foresee it. He added that such reactions only highlight why stories like Chiraiya are important.

general news.headline: Iran closes Hormuz again after Israeli attacks on Lebanon despite ceasefireIran closes Strait of Hormuz again after Israeli attacks on Lebanon kill hundreds: State media

Live: Truce in Lebanon essential condition in US deal, Iran President tells Pak PMUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: The Middle East slipped back into instability as Lebanon witnessed some of the heaviest attack by Israel despite a two-week truce with US, with over 100 people killed and around 700 injured. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu made it clear that Lebanon is not part of the agreement; even as the United States and Iran announced a pause in attacks, fresh strikes were reported across Iran, the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain, raising serious doubts over the truce's durability, while US President Donald Trump said the ceasefire — brokered with Pakistan's involvement — includes Tehran allowing safe transit through the Strait of Hormuz, with talks scheduled in Islamabad on Friday to decide the next steps. Iran has claimed victory but stressed that the upcoming negotiations do not signal the end of the war. Trump indicated possible sanctions and tariff relief for Tehran while reiterating that Iran will not be allowed to enrich uranium and warning of steep tariffs on countries supplying weapons to Iran, even as oil prices eased following the announcement, with Brent crude slipping below $95 a barrel. Catch all the live updates at Indiatoday.in.

Iran's oil refinery hit; missiles pound UAE, Kuwait, Lebanon despite ceasefireA wave of fresh attacks – from Iran's own oil infrastructure being hit to the UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain coming under Iranian missile and drone fire and Israeli strikes intensifying in Lebanon – cast immediate doubt over whether the two-week ceasefire can hold.

War not over but Trump talks of joint venture with Iran to collect Hormuz tollUS President Donald Trump has floated the idea of a joint venture with Iran on the Strait of Hormuz to collect tolls from ships. Trump's remarks on "big money" by "just hanging around" came within hours of the US's ceasefire with Iran.

I stopped using Blinkit and Instamart for 30 days. My wallet (and health) thanked meI deleted Blinkit and Instamart from my phone just to see if I could survive in this digital-first world. It seems, I thrived.

Did White House push Pakistan to broker ceasefire before Iran deadline?The Trump administration leaned on Pakistan to persuade Iran to accept a pause in fighting. The proposal focused on reopening the Strait of Hormuz. Pakistan's role was to present the deal as coming from a fellow Muslim-majority state, in the hope it would be more acceptable to Tehran.

Middle East on fire again: A bilateral ceasefire in multilateral war falls apartHours after the ceasefire between the US and Iran was announced, several airstrikes have been reported from the Middle East. From Iran to the UAE to Kuwait, Bahrain, Saudi Arabia, several energy sites are in flames. A bilateral ceasefire cannot help in a multilateral war.

Fight isn't fair, you f*** off from there: Samay Raina's viral Kashmiri Pandit wisdomWe have compiled comedian Samay Raina's five statements from the show Still Alive that made the audience laugh, pause, reflect, and, at times, even tear up.

Amity, Ashoka join IITs in shaping the Cognitive Science revolution, driving Artificial IntelligenceCognitive science is, by its very nature, a field that requires the simultaneous presence of psychology, neuroscience, computer science, linguistics and philosophy.

DC vs GT Live: Onus on Rahul after Rashid double-strikeDelhi Capitals (DC) vs Gujarat Titans (GT) Live Score, IPL 2026: DC (118/3 in 12 ov), Rashid Khan's double-strike has rocked DC's run chase at the Arun Jaitley stadium. The GT spinner gets rid of Nitish Rana and the in-form Sameer Rizvi to get GT back into the game.

TVK loses one seat even before its debut contest in Tamil Nadu pollsThe nomination of a TVK candidate from the Edappadi constituency has been rejected, and the candidate has now gone missing, with the party alleging kidnapping.

Identity of Satoshi Nakamoto revealed? New report says Bitcoin creator is…Who is Satoshi Nakamoto, the person who created Bitcoin? This has been one of the biggest mysteries in the crypto and tech world. Now, a new report suggests that all clues lead to a British technologist even though the person has denied it again and again.

Iran sets $1 per barrel crypto toll on tankers transiting via Strait of Hormuz: FTUnder the proposed system, tankers would be required to share cargo details via email. Authorities would then calculate a toll -- set at $1 per barrel -- to be paid in digital currencies such as bitcoin. Hosseini noted that vessels would be given only a short window to complete payment.

Amazon responds after reports claim it may lay off 14,000 employees next monthAmazon is reportedly planning for another major round of layoffs. The American giant may fire as many as 14,000 workers across divisions globally with some teams possibly shutting down. The company has cut 16,000 roles in January this year.

Why did Iran fire a baby pink missile at Tel Aviv? Because a baby girl wanted soAmid the brutal US-Israel-Iran war, violence was delivered in pastel pink. Why? Because a little girl asked the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) to "plough Tel Aviv" with a pink missile.

Democrats want the President out. But can Donald Trump be impeached?Even as the US's war with Iran entered a fragile ceasefire, Democrats are raring up to impeach President Donald Trump. A sitting US President can be removed in at least two different ways. But the question is, can Trump actually be removed at the present because of the mess he created in the Middle East?

Get permit or you will be destroyed: Iran Navy's message to tankers crossing HormuzThe Iranian navy has warned that any vessel attempting to pass through the strait without prior approval could be targeted and destroyed, signalling that transit remains tightly restricted despite the truce.

No uranium enrichment, we'll talk tariff and sanctions relief with Iran: TrumpTrump says US will seek zero uranium enrichment from Iran while negotiating tariff cuts and sanctions relief after ceasefire; claims parts of a broader deal already agreed.

How 'farcical', 'b***s**t' Israeli intel led Trump to Iran warThe origins of the Iran conflict can be traced back to a closed-door briefing in the White House that was attended by Israeli PM Netanyahu, Trump and his aides. After the meeting, several of Trump's aides, including Marco Rubio and JD Vance, were not on the same page regarding a military campaign in Iran.

Minerva's historic win vs Liverpool: A Spain trip that almost did not happenMinerva Academy kids created history when they beat Liverpool U15s 6-0 in the MIC Cup, a prestigious international youth tournament. Here's the untold story of how the historic trip almost did not happen.

Meet Claude Mythos, Anthropic AI so powerful it can hack any softwareAnthropic has a new AI model, called Claude Mythos Preview, which is so good that the AI startup has no plans of making it available to the public anytime soon. Instead, Claude Mythos Preview will be made available to a consortium of over 40 tech companies, such as Apple and Google, that will use the model to fix potential cybersecurity vulnerabilities.

Sir, I'll tell you everything: Constable key to death penalty for 9 Tamil Nadu copsHead Constable Revathi's courageous testimony, defying threats and institutional pressure, proved pivotal in securing death sentences for nine Tamil Nadu police personnel five years after a father and son died in custodial torture.

Called auntie by a co-worker, Indian-origin NHS worker in UK wins £1,425 payoutAn Indian-origin healthcare assistant in the UK working with the NHS has been awarded £1,425 by the Employment Tribunal after ruling she had been harassed by a colleague who called her an "auntie". Healthcare assistant Ilda Esteves had complained that, despite multiple requests, her colleague refused to call her by her name.

sports.headline: Billie Jean King Cup: Vaishnavi, Sahaja power India to 3-0 sweep over New Zealand Young debutant Vaishnavi Adkar and Sahaja Yamalapalli secured decisive victories as India bounced back to blank New Zealand 3-0 in their second Asia/Oceania Group I tie of the Billie Jean King Cup on Wednesday.

Mohammed Shami devising new variations with Bharat Arun to outfox IPL batters Mohammed Shami outfoxed Abhishek Sharma with a new delivery he had developed just days earlier while working with bowling coach Bharat Arun, Lucknow Super Giants spin bowling coach Carl Crowe revealed.

Cameron Green set to make his bowling return during KKR vs LSG clash in Kolkata Cameron Green is all set to make his bowling return in the IPL during the KKR vs LSG clash at the Eden Gardens on Thursday, April 9. Green is yet to bowl in the IPL this season in three matches so far as he has failed to justify his price tag so far.

Doha Diamond League postponed until June due to West Asia tensions The opening round of the Diamond League season, scheduled to be held in Doha on May 8, has been postponed to June 19 due to the ongoing conflict in the Gulf, organisers said on Wednesday.

Will Narine and Varun return for LSG clash? KKR sweat over key duo's fitness KKR have issued an update on Sunil Narine and Varun Chakravarthy after the spin duo missed the last clash against Punjab Kings in Kolkata. Narine and Chakravarthy lead KKR's spin attack this season.

David Warner's alcohol admission surfaces in Sydney drink driving investigation Former Australian opener David Warner has reportedly told police that he had been drinking with a friend before allegedly being caught for drink-driving in Sydney.

Watch: Medvedev loses cool, smashes racket seven times in heavy loss to Berrettini World No. 10 Daniil Medvedev endured one of the most humiliating defeats of his career, going down 0-6, 0-6 to Matteo Berrettini in the third round of the Monte-Carlo Masters. Medvedev, visibly rattled throughout the contest, smashed his racquet multiple times in frustration.

The GT spinner gets rid of Nitish Rana and the in-form Sameer Rizvi to get GT back into the game.

The KKR captain highlighted the hard work behind his T20 evolution while acknowledging the team's early-season scrutiny.

Smriti Mandhana-Palash Mucchal patch-up? Viral family meeting video sparks buzz A viral video showing Smriti Mandhana's father with Palak Muchhal and Mithoon has sparked speculation about a possible patch-up between the Indian cricketer and Palash Muchhal after their reported split in 2025.

DC vs GT: Will rain play spoilsport in 3rd straight IPL game?Two back to back matches in the Indian Premier League have been interrupted by rain. Will it be the same for Delhi Capitals vs Gujarat Titans as well?

R Ashwin backs Yashasvi Jaiswal to get Orange Cap in IPL 2026Ravichandran Ashwin has backed Yashasvi Jaiswal to be the leading contender for Orange Cap after his explosive start to the Indian Premier League 2026 season. Jaiswal's consistent, high strike-rate performances have put him ahead early.

CSK CEO updates on MS Dhoni's injury: Will he play team's next match vs DC?Chennai Super Kings will continue to miss MS Dhoni in their next IPL 2026 game. CSK CEO confirmed the news, stating that Dhoni was yet to return to full fitness from his calf injury.

Is Fleming the problem for CSK? Ex-India star says coach dependent on DhoniIPL 2026: Stephen Fleming finds himself under pressure as CSK's struggles continue, with questions now being asked about his impact without MS Dhoni leading the side. As losses pile up, the spotlight has firmly shifted from players to the dugout.

Badminton Asia Champs: Lakshya Sen crashes out in first round, Sindhu advancesIndia had a horror start in the Badminton Asia Championships after Lakshya Sen crashed out against Hong Kong China's Lee Cheuk Yiu in straight games. India's challenge in the competition remains alive as Ayush Shetty and PV Sindhu advanced to the next round.

Bayern's 2014 Neuer comes out of syllabus for Real Madrid in UCL defeatBayern Munich secured a 2–1 win over Real Madrid at the Bernabéu, but it was 40-year-old Manuel Neuer who stole the spotlight with a sensational nine-save performance. Despite Bayern's dominance and missed chances, Madrid remain firmly in the tie ahead of the second leg in Munich.

Beyond cricket's billions: Why hockey's 'El Dorado' lies in speed, not sprawlAs the IPL cements its status as a billion-dollar spectacle, Indian hockey finds itself battling irrelevance, caught between nostalgia and a failure to adapt to modern sport's demands. Can hockey make a comeback in an era of constant visibility, monetisation, and reinvention?

When will J&K pacer Auqib Nabi make his IPL debut?IPL 2026: Delhi Capitals are set to play Gujarat Titans at their own backyard on Wednesday, April 8. Despite acquiring Jammu and Kashmir pacer Auqib Nabi for Rs 8.40 crore, Delhi have not handed him a debut yet in the IPL.

DC vs GT: Gill back for struggling Gujarat as Delhi eye a fortress at KotlaIPL, DC vs GT Preview: Delhi Capitals look to build on their strong start at home as they chase a hat-trick of wins. Shubman Gill's return, meanwhile, offers a timely boost to a struggling Gujarat Titans side.

Bindaas Vaibhav Sooryavanshi forcing Jaiswal to level-up at Rajasthan RoyalsIPL, RR vs MI: Yashasvi Jaiswal and Vaibhav Sooryavanshi ripped apart Mumbai Indians in Guwahati on April 7. After the win, Jaiswal spoke about how young Sooryavanshi was forcing him to level up at Rajasthan Royals.

Sooryvanshi's evolution will be fun to watch: MI coach Jayawardane on RR starboyIPL 2026: Mahela Jayawardene praised Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless knock against MI, calling his evolution exciting to watch. The 15-year-old stunned Jasprit Bumrah with two sixes, continuing his formidable start to the season.

IPL Play Of The Day: MI's big guns fail to replicate Jaiswal-Sooryavanshi blitzIPL 2026: On a rainy day which saw the game being reduced to 11 overs, RR sent out Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal to play a bit of contra on the field and shoot at everything. But when it came to MI, the approach seemed to lack the intent needed to pull off a big chase.

HD Throwback: Why Vaibhav Sooryavanshi vs Bumrah felt like Sachin vs QadirIPL 2026: 37 years after Sachin Tendulkar's sixes against Abdul Qadir in Peshawar, history finds a high-definition mirror in Guwahati. Vaibhav Sooryavanshi's fearless takedown of Jasprit Bumrah captured the same rare, transcendent magic as that legendary 1989 encounter on Tuesday.

IPL 2026: How Buttler is trusting his game and experience for GT form revivalJos Buttler is backing his own game and experience to turn things around in the IPL with the Gujarat Titans after a poor 2026 in T20Is. Buttler has had a decent start to his IPL 2026 campaign but is yet to contribute a big total.

Storm after rain: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal blow MI awayIPL 2026, RR vs MI: Rain couldn't stop the Rajasthan Royals' momentum as Vaibhav Sooryavanshi and Yashasvi Jaiswal turned an 11-over shootout into a masterclass in carnage. Sinking Mumbai Indians by 27 runs in Guwahati, the 2008 champions remain flawless in IPL 2026, reclaiming the top spot with their third consecutive win.

Candidates chess: Praggnanandhaa loses, almost out of contention for top placeIndian Grandmaster R Praggnanandhaa suffered a setback in the Candidates tournament after losing to Anish Giri in the eighth round in Chennai. The loss has hurt his chances of finishing at the top.

Ravi Shastri stand to be unveiled at Wankhede, MCA to honour Mumbai greatsThe Mumbai cricket association has decided to unveil the Ravi Shastri stand at the Wankhede Stadium on Thursday, April 8. Along with Shastri, Diana Edulji, Dilip Sardesai will also be honoured by the MCA.

Mohammed Shami should play for India again: Ganguly sends reminder to AgarkarIPL 2026: Former India captain Sourav Ganguly has firmly backed Mohammed Shami's return to the senior national team, stating that the 35-year-old pacer remains supremely fit and hungry to keep adding to his wicket tally. Shami has been in impressive form for Lucknow Super Giants in the ongoing IPL season.

Vaibhav Sooryavanshi stuns Bumrah with first-ball six, MI ace can only smileVaibhav Sooryavanshi showed no fear against Jasprit Bumrah and dispatched the Indian star pacer for a first-ball six in Guwahati.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
